title: Vegetarianism
questions: What comes to mind when you hear the word 'vegetarianism'?
Would the world be a better place if everyone was a vegetarian?
Why do people become vegetarians?
Do you think vegetarians lead better lives than non-vegetarians?
Is there anything unhealthy about a vegetarian diet?
What three adjectives would you use to describe vegetarianism?
What would happen to the cow population if we stopped eating them?
Albert Einstein said: "Nothing will benefit human health and increase chances for survival of life on Earth as much as the evolution to a vegetarian diet." Do you agree?
Someone once asked: "If vegetarians eat vegetables, what do humanitarians eat?" What do you think?
Margi Clark said: "I think if you want to eat more meat you should kill it yourself and eat it raw." What do you think?
What do you know about vegetarianism?
What are the arguments against vegetarianism?
What do you think vegetarians think of meat eaters?
Would we help save the planet if we were all vegetarian?
Is a vegetarian diet healthier than a meat diet?
What's a strict vegetarian?
Will we all be vegetarians in the future?
Gandhi said: "To my mind, the life of a lamb is no less precious than that of a human being. I should be unwilling to take the life of a lamb for the sake of the human body." What do you think?
K.D. Lang asked: "We all love animals. Why do we call some "pets" and others "dinner?" What do you think?
Paul McCartney said: "If slaughterhouses had glass walls, everyone would be a vegetarian." What do you think?
